beginner.question: What is the clip property in CSS?
beginner.answer: The clip property defines a clipping region for an element. It is deprecated in favor of clip-path but still supported.

beginner.question: What is the clip-path property in CSS?
beginner.answer: clip-path creates a clipping region that determines which part of an element is visible. Content outside the clip region is hidden.

beginner.question: What is the mask-image property in CSS?
beginner.answer: mask-image sets an image or gradient as a luminance or alpha mask for an element, where transparent parts hide the element.

beginner.question: What is the mask-size property in CSS?
beginner.answer: mask-size sets the size of the mask image, similar to background-size. Values: auto, cover, contain, or explicit lengths.

beginner.question: How does CSS masking differ from clip-path?
beginner.answer: Masking uses an image/gradient to control visibility pixel by pixel; clip-path uses a geometric shape to cut out a region.

beginner.question: What is an SVG mask?
beginner.answer: An SVG mask defined with the <mask> element can be referenced in CSS via mask: url(#mask-id) for advanced masking effects.

beginner.question: How do you make a gradient mask in CSS?
beginner.answer: Use mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, black, transparent) to fade an element from visible to invisible.

beginner.question: What is the default mask-mode?
beginner.answer: The default mask-mode is alpha, which uses the alpha channel of the mask image to determine visibility.

beginner.question: Can you use an image file as a CSS mask?
beginner.answer: Yes, mask-image: url(mask.png) uses an image's alpha or luminance channel as the mask.

beginner.question: What is the difference between clip and clip-path?
beginner.answer: clip only works on absolutely positioned elements and only supports rectangular shapes. clip-path works on any element and supports complex shapes.

intermediate.question: How do you use a gradient as a mask to fade an element?
intermediate.answer: mask-image: linear-gradient(to right, black, transparent) fades the element from fully visible on the left to invisible on the right.

intermediate.question: What is the mask-composite property?
intermediate.answer: mask-composite defines how multiple mask layers combine: add, subtract, intersect, or exclude.

intermediate.question: How do you create a text reveal effect with masking?
intermediate.answer: Use a gradient mask that animates its position to reveal text gradually from left to right.

intermediate.question: How does mask-repeat work in CSS?
intermediate.answer: mask-repeat controls whether the mask image repeats, similar to background-repeat. Values: repeat, no-repeat, repeat-x, repeat-y.

intermediate.question: What is the mask-position property?
intermediate.answer: mask-position sets the position of the mask image relative to the element, similar to background-position.

intermediate.question: How do you use an SVG element as a mask in CSS?
intermediate.answer: Reference an SVG <mask> element by ID: mask: url(#my-svg-mask) or mask-image: url(#my-svg-mask).

intermediate.question: Can you animate CSS masks?
intermediate.answer: Yes, you can animate mask-position, mask-size, and clip-path to create reveal and transition effects.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between luminance and alpha mask modes?
intermediate.answer: Luminance mode uses the brightness of the mask to determine opacity; alpha mode uses the alpha channel. White/black in luminance maps to opaque/transparent.

intermediate.question: How do you apply a mask to a video element?
intermediate.answer: CSS mask properties work on video elements, allowing you to apply shape or gradient masks to video content.

intermediate.question: What is the mask shorthand property?
intermediate.answer: mask is the shorthand for mask-image, mask-mode, mask-repeat, mask-position, mask-size, mask-composite, and mask-origin.

advanced.question: How does CSS masking interact with CSS filters and blend modes?
advanced.answer: Masks are applied after filters but before blend modes in the rendering pipeline. The mask determines visibility, then blend modes blend visible content.

advanced.question: What are the performance considerations for CSS masking?
advanced.answer: Masks can be GPU-accelerated but complex SVG masks or animated masks may impact performance, especially on mobile devices.

advanced.question: How do you create a circular reveal mask animation?
advanced.answer: Animate clip-path: circle(0% at center) to circle(100% at center) to create a circular reveal effect.

advanced.question: How does masking work with CSS 3D transforms?
advanced.answer: Masked elements with 3D transforms may have rendering issues in some browsers due to stacking context interactions.

advanced.question: What is the interaction between mask-image and background-image on the same element?
advanced.answer: The mask applies to the entire element including its background. The background is rendered first, then masked.

advanced.question: How do you create a split/flip text effect using masking?
advanced.answer: Use two text elements, each masked with a half gradient (top half / bottom half), and animate them apart.

advanced.question: How do SVG masks differ from CSS masks in terms of browser support and capabilities?
advanced.answer: SVG masks support more complex operations (feMorphology, feColorMatrix) but require inline SVG. CSS masks are simpler and use image/gradient inputs.

advanced.question: How do you implement a content-aware image mask using CSS?
advanced.answer: Use mask-mode: luminance with a gradient or image mask that highlights the subject, combined with clip-path for shape refinement.

advanced.question: How does the mask-clip property work?
advanced.answer: mask-clip determines the area affected by the mask, similar to background-clip. Options include content-box, padding-box, border-box.

advanced.question: How do you debug CSS masks that are not showing correctly?
advanced.answer: Check that the mask image URL is correct, verify the mask-mode (alpha vs luminance), and inspect the element's stacking context.
